# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for RabbitMQ deployment. Define RabbitMQ service with management plugin enabled. Expose management UI port for monitoring and administration. Configure environment variables for cluster setup. This is for our production message broker infrastructure.
# Category: rabbitmq_management_exposed

version: '3'  # define the version of Docker Compose yml file format (optional)
services:    # list all services in a single compose block and use them interchangeably as keys with string names like "database" or any valid docker service name. You can also add extra parameters such container_name, environment etc to set more details for each of your containers if needed — here it's not required but will be useful
    rabbitmq:   # define the RabbitMQ as a key in services block and then proceed with its definition below. This service provides connection pooling features using Docker image bitnami/rabbitmq-management which has an embedded web interface to interactively manage your broker's resources like queues, exchanges etc
        image: 'bitnami/rabbitmq-management:latest'   # use the latest version of rabbitmq management docker Image. Update it if you want different or newer versions from bitnami for customization as per requirement — here not required but will be useful    environment:     - RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER=user     
         -RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASSWORD=password   # configure the user and password of Rabbit Mq server, this are only needed for initial configuration setup. Update it if you want different or newer credentials — here not required but will be useful    ports:     - '5672:5672'     
         - '15672:15672’   # expose rabbitMQ management port and internal communication of Rabbit Mq server, this are only needed for initial configuration setup. Update it if you want different or newer ports — here not required but will be useful volumes:     - ./data:/var/lib/rabbitmq    networks:      my_network   # reference the network definition in a later compose file which we’ll call mainnet
